text:
In a tiny village on the edge of a rainforest, there lived an owl. The owl had piercing eyes that could see colors other creatures couldn't imagine. One morning, while chasing after a curious caterpillar, the owl discovered something remarkable.

The forest was buzzing with life. Birds flew overhead singing, insects buzzed around like lost bees. But there was something different. Some flowers seemed awake. They swayed gently. They moved towards each other.

Word spread through the forest like wildfire. The animals came — birds landing gracefully on branches, rodents nibbling hungrily. Even creatures the owl hadn't spoken to before stopped. They listened.

That afternoon, under an old oak tree famous among legends for its dreams, the owl shared its wonder. It spoke of dreams so vivid they seemed real. Hawks soared together. Frogs croaked melodies. Even the gentlest things touched hearts.

That night, under the same old oak tree, something magical happened. Flowers bloomed wider. Trees whispered secrets
